real_architecture_summary: Live startup flows through project/scripts/run_live_engine.py into LiveEngineRunner, which owns an OMS, kill-switch logic, health checks, and periodic account sync. The proactive trading gate is split from reactive kill-switch and accounting paths. Runtime postflight/replay checks are batch artifacts that do not always exercise the same normalization path as live runtime.
top_invariants: Monitor-only mode must prevent all venue mutation, including kill-switch unwinds.
Trading mode must require validated strategy/runtime provenance, not a bare implemented flag.
Safety-critical logic must not depend on stale LiveStateStore snapshots when fills are local and snapshots are delayed.

findings.title: Monitor-only runners can still send real flatten orders when the kill-switch fires
findings.evidence: build_live_runner() creates an exchange-backed OMS in monitor_only when credentials exist, and _handle_kill_switch_trigger() always calls cancel_all_orders() and flatten_all_positions(), which submit reduce_only market orders.
findings.why_it_matters: A supposedly non-trading observer can place real venue orders during a stale-feed or health-triggered unwind.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Instantiate LiveEngineRunner(runtime_mode="monitor_only") with an exchange-backed OrderManager and positions, then await _handle_kill_switch_trigger().
findings.suggested_remediation: Hard-gate all venue mutation paths on runtime_mode, or rename/document the mode if protective order placement is intentional.

findings.title: Monitor-only startup still requires a tradable Binance account and trading credentials
findings.evidence: main() always runs preflight_binance_venue_connectivity() and validate_binance_account_preflight() regardless of runtime_mode, while config contracts label live_paper/live_production as monitor_only.
findings.why_it_matters: There is no safe read-only deployment tier despite the naming and config contract.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Supply a non-tradable account preflight payload to validate_binance_account_preflight() while using a monitor_only config.
findings.suggested_remediation: Branch startup requirements on runtime_mode and allow monitor_only to use read-only credentials/connectivity checks.

findings.title: Incubation or paper gating is ineffective and graduation status is inverted
findings.evidence: _prepare_strategy_order() only writes order.metadata["is_paper"], submit_order_async() ignores it, and IncubationLedger.is_graduated() returns False even after graduate() sets status="live".
findings.why_it_matters: Incubating strategies are not truly kept out of live trading, and explicit graduation status is misreported.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Exercise IncubationLedger.graduate() and submit a non-graduated strategy order through a venue-backed OMS.
findings.suggested_remediation: Fix is_graduated() semantics and enforce incubation at the OMS submission boundary.

findings.title: Any caller can forge a StrategyResult and reach live trading by flipping strategy_runtime.implemented
findings.evidence: submit_strategy_result_async() accepts arbitrary StrategyResult-like payloads, and _ensure_trading_enabled() only checks runtime_mode and strategy_runtime["implemented"]. build_live_order_from_strategy_result() derives orders directly from result.data.
findings.why_it_matters: Unsafe or fabricated runtime objects can bypass provenance checks and create venue orders.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Pass a handcrafted StrategyResult with target_position and fill_price into submit_strategy_result_async() on a trading runner.
findings.suggested_remediation: Bind live-trading eligibility to validated runtime object type or signed strategy provenance, not a free-form implemented flag.

findings.title: Runtime postflight audit can return pass for malformed event rows because normalization is skipped
findings.evidence: run_runtime_postflight_audit() never calls normalize_event_rows(); it counts raw rows, and run_watermark_audit() skips rows missing timestamps.
findings.why_it_matters: Replay/postflight artifacts can go green on malformed runtime data that the real normalization stage would reject or truncate.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Call run_runtime_postflight_audit() on an events_df missing timestamp fields and inspect the returned status and normalization counts.
findings.suggested_remediation: Normalize first or consume the normalized replay artifact before performing postflight checks.

findings.title: OMS fills do not update LiveStateStore, so safety decisions run on stale account state until the next snapshot sync
findings.evidence: on_order_fill() updates the OMS and execution-quality report, but it does not mutate LiveStateStore; account state only changes on periodic exchange snapshots.
findings.why_it_matters: Sizing, drawdown checks, and kill-switch logic can act on stale balances and positions between exchange syncs.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Call on_order_fill() on a runner with an in-memory order and inspect state_store.account before and after.
findings.suggested_remediation: Apply provisional local state updates on fills or maintain a merged local execution ledger for safety-critical logic.

test_coverage_assessment: Live tests cover order lifecycle and some kill-switch behavior, but not monitor_only kill-switch mutation, non-trading startup expectations, incubating-strategy enforcement, forged StrategyResult provenance, fill-to-state-store accounting, or malformed-row postflight behavior.
docs_specs_code_contradictions: live_* configs are labeled monitor_only, but startup and kill-switch paths still behave like trading-capable flows.
run_runtime_postflight_audit reports normalization fields without performing the real normalization step.
suggested_regression_tests: A non-tradable-account startup test for monitor_only.
open_questions_or_unknowns: Whether monitor_only was intentionally meant to permit protective venue mutation for externally held positions.
